M3 vision: dim warm interior, gallery wall of framed photos (matches Michelin fact 'walls covered with Judaica and photos of Israeli markets'), burgundy banquette, globe pendant lights. A server in a white shirt + tie stands at center serving a table of diners; foreground-left and foreground-right diners are clearly recognizable faces. Strongest atmospheric image, but the people-forward composition and identifiable faces make it gallery_atmosphere, not a clean architectural hero.

M3 vision: top-down overhead shot of two plated dishes on marble surface (roasted aubergine/zucchini on patterned platter, smoked salmon on blini with cream). Marble visible at the edges hints at upscale, but no interior, no people, no seating. Editorial food photography from CNT review. Cannot serve as interior or hero — useful as a product_context reference only.

Don Julio Parrilla.readiness_reason: Both Don Julio candidates on disk failed the resolution gate (<1024px) and were not vision-analyzed. The 3rd M2.7 candidate (Argentina Tourism) is missing from disk and M3 cannot scout per constraints. Don Julio has zero M3-verified interior candidates from the M2.7 queue. Don Julio requires either (a) re-download of the same URLs at higher resolution, or (b) new editorial sources with confirmed full-res availability, before it can be staged.

Mishiguene.readiness_reason: candidate_5 (CNT, 2560x1440) is the only M3-verified gallery candidate: real Mishiguene interior (gallery wall + banquette + globe lights matching Michelin + 50 Best descriptions). However: (a) it is editorial / Condé Nast controlled (rights_review_needed), (b) contains 3+ identifiable faces including a server and foreground diners (face_release_needed, identity_review_needed), (c) people-forward composition rules it out as a clean architectural hero. Use as gallery_atmosphere with proper attribution; do NOT use as the venue's hero card without face releases.

Mili\u00f3n.readiness_reason: candidate_6 (CNT) is the only vision-analyzed image and it is a top-down product shot of two plates on marble — not interior, not seating, not hero. The venue-controlled s1.png / milion.com.ar home strip is 600x295 and excluded as thumb. Milion has zero M3-verified interior candidates from the M2.7 queue. To get a real hero/gallery image for Milion requires either (a) full-res version of the official site gallery (s2-s6b), (b) Wander Argentina images at full resolution, or (c) new editorial sources.

findings: M2.7 prevision identified 9 high-editorial candidates superior to TripAdvisor UGC. Confirmed by re-reading the preflight: 100% editorial sourcing (Michelin, 50 Best, Argentina Tourism, CNT, milion.com.ar).

BUT 7 of 9 (78%) are not vision-analyzable as-is: 6 fail the resolution gate (<1024px) and 1 is missing from disk. This is HIGHER than the 22% exclusion rate measured on the prior BA Ninina/Verne/Mishiguene run (2026-06-05, 9-image queue, 5 venues).

The jump from 22% to 78% exclusion is explained by source: editorial sites (Michelin, 50 Best) serve small gallery thumbs when an HTML scraper pulls them at default size, vs. self-hosted WP/Shopify content (Ninina 1066, Verne 2048, prior Mishiguene 1536-2048) which serves full-res by default.
M2.7 prevision is honest about URL/class but does NOT have file size or actual dimensions — only filename-declared dimensions, which we already established in skill pitfall #11 are unreliable.

M3 vision verdict is consistent with M2.7's prevision for the 2 images that could be analyzed: candidate_5 was previsioned as 'interior_possible' and IS a real interior; candidate_6 was previsioned as 'venue / atmosphere' but is actually a product-only top-down shot. M3 corrected 1/2 (50% prevision accuracy on this batch, vs 11% measured on the Ninina/Verne batch).

The skill's recommended split (M2.7 text/URL → M3 vision) is still the right structure. The unresolved piece is: between M2.7 and M3, a higher-resolution re-fetch step is needed for editorial sources. M2.7 prevision cannot solve this from URL patterns alone.
pipeline_validation.recommended_next_step: Before running a Batch 02 with the same 3 venues, either (a) add a re-fetch step to get full-res versions of the editorial candidates (Michelin gallery, 50 Best, CNT), or (b) extend M2.7 to record filename-declared dimensions AND a content-length HEAD request, and skip candidates where declared_dim < 1024 OR content-length < 50KB. The split structure (M2.7 text → M3 vision) is validated, the prevision accuracy is acceptable (50%+), but the pre-vision resolution gate must run against actual files, not URL patterns.

notes: All 8 present files were magic-byte-validated (PIL.open + format check). All 8 are valid JPG/PNG. No corrupted files.

candidate_7 and milion_s1.png are byte-identical (md5 156c024d). The M2.7 preflight lists these as 2 separate queue entries (priority 1 and priority 3 for Milión) but they collapse to 1 unique image. M3 dedupes correctly.
M3 vision was not called on any sub-1024 image. Per skill pitfall #11, calling vision on thumbs wastes tokens for no decision-grade information.

No M3 vision call was made for any image NOT in the M2.7 preflight queue. The gate2_* and dj_cnt_donjulio_og.jpg files in /home/hermes/.chrome_tmp/vision_queue/ are from a separate experiment and were not touched.
No DB write, no Cloudinary upload, no publish action, no Korantis file modification. Phase B was read-only against the disk artifacts and JSON output.
